dependencies: Coal supply from various geographic areas under long-term contracts
Natural gas supply from Appalachian, Gulf Coast, Mid-Continent, Canadian, and Michigan regions
Dependence on interstate pipelines for natural gas transportation
risks: Volatility in commodity markets and fuel supply disruptions
Changes in cost and availability of coal, uranium, purchased power, and natural gas
Fuel supply disruptions could negatively impact operations
Price fluctuations and changes in transportation costs driven by inflation
Concentration risk in supply chain for certain commodities

regulatory_risks: Impact of regulation by EPA, EGLE, FERC, MPSC, NRC, CFTC, and CARB
Changes in federal nuclear regulation
Environmental laws and regulations increasing costs of remediation and compliance
New federal and state environmental requirements
Uncertainty around future environmental regulations affecting long-term capital planning
Coal Combustion Residuals (CCR) and Effluent Limitations Guidelines (ELG) rules
Clean Energy Standard requiring 100% clean energy portfolio by 2040
Renewable Portfolio Standard requiring 50% renewable energy by 2030 and 60% by 2035
Energy waste reduction requirements
Retail access program regulations and potential changes
Rate regulation by MPSC and FERC
Regulatory proceedings and appeals affecting cost recovery
Potential disallowance of costs in customer rates by regulators
Changes in Michigan's electric retail access program
Federal and state fuel standards affecting non-utility investments
Terrorism Risk Insurance Program Reauthorization Act (TRIA)
Price-Anderson Amendments Act for nuclear liability
Clean Water Act section 316(b) and 316(a) regulations
National Ambient Air Quality Standards (NAAQS) for fine particulate matter
EPA rules addressing greenhouse gas emissions from power sector
Good Neighbor Rule affecting compressor engines for natural gas transportation
State Implementation Plans (SIP) for non-attainment areas
Federal Implementation Plan (FIP) for SO2 non-attainment areas
